chapter_title.ar: باب وَقْتِ الْمَغْرِبِ وَ الْعِشَاءِ الْآخِرَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 6 – The time for Al Maghrib and the last Isha (Salāts)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/41)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/41)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لَوْ لَا أَنْ أَشُقَّ عَلَى أُمَّتِي لَأَخَّرْتُ الْعِشَاءَ إِلَى ثُلُثِ اللَّيْلِ .
وَ رُوِيَ أَيْضاً إِلَى نِصْفِ اللَّيْلِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhmmad, from Al Washa, from Aban, from Abu Baseer,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘Had it not been grievous upon mysaww community, Isaww would have delayed Al-Isha (Salāt) up to a third of the night’.
And it is reported as well (that he<sup>saww</sup> said): ‘Up to half the night’.
en.sarwar: 13. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Washsha’ from Aban from abu Basir from who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah has said, ‘If it was not because of al-Shafaq (bright redness), I would have delayed al-‘Isha’ Salah (prayer) up till passing of one-third of night.’” It is also narrated, “up till midnight.’”
